tasks.title: Run scanner contract validation suite
tasks.verification: Recorded clean checkpoint after trunk merge compatibility resolution. Merge commit 124d94aa95b5111b8f2b3fde012a262093eb451a includes origin/trunk and resolved ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md conflict. Verification after merge passed: targeted scanner/manifest/phantom suite (163 tests) and pnpm run check.
tasks.notes: Completed final scanner contract validation. Checkpoint clean at 11978a2bfc53c74575a56a59ce6199791e57a757.

wisdom.content: For targeted Vitest runs in this repo, `pnpm exec vitest run src/<file>.test.ts` gives isolated file execution; `pnpm test -- src/<file>.test.ts` can surface unrelated high-signal failures through the package script/tool output path, so use the exact vitest command when validating a single asset test.

Scanner contract changes are efficient when implemented as spec/command/skill/docs + asset-test anchors in one vertical slice per scanner requirement. The existing `*-assets.test.ts` pattern is enough to lock requirement IDs, command anchors, and skill methodology without adding a new runtime subsystem.

Manifest/doc drift tests already enforce scanner description consistency across manifest, command frontmatter, README, and ADV_INSTRUCTIONS. For discoverability changes, add a focused manifest expectation first, then rely on `manifest-doc-drift.test.ts` to propagate exact wording.

For scanner contract changes, a compact final verification suite can combine the two scanner asset tests with manifest and manifest-doc drift tests, then `pnpm run check` for full type/lint/format coverage. This catches spec-command-skill-doc drift without needing the full Vitest suite.

Executive summary persisted and verified. Contract matrix has 19 required rows passed/respected, 0 failing/unknown. Verification passed: targeted scanner/manifest suite (73 tests) and pnpm run check. Worktree clean at checkpoint 315d1c4bd9e83ecdc5de8d546d804d915500ff59.

items.text: `/adv-slop-scan` defines bloat/removal categories: unused dependency/export/file, unreachable branch, uncallable private symbol, impossible feature-flag path.
Uncertain deletion candidates appear in a separate low-confidence/user-review section.
`/adv-slop-scan` never treats heuristic-only or text-only unused-code guesses as actionable removal proof.
`/adv-arch-scan` supports stack rule packs before generic heuristic fallback.
Initial packs cover ADV’s known stack; newly detected relevant stack pieces dynamically get a pack or explicit missing-pack coverage.
Skipped/degraded detectors and missing packs are always summarized, with detail in JSON/metadata.
Specs and asset tests cover all new scanner contracts.
Existing guarantees remain intact: AST-first/degraded annotations, source evidence, low-confidence grouping, context-window suppression.
Specs remain laws; scanner contract changes must be reflected in `.adv/specs/*` and docs/spec mirrors where applicable.
Findings must be evidence-backed with file/source/tool citations.

Public exports, generated files, tests, fixtures, command modules, plugin registration surfaces, prompt context, examples, and task summaries are high false-positive or non-scannable surfaces unless target source evidence proves otherwise.
Coverage gaps must be visible without forcing users into verbose mode, but detailed payloads should live in JSON/metadata to avoid bloating normal output.
Newly detected relevant stack pieces should dynamically get a pack when practical, or an explicit missing-pack/degraded coverage signal when not practical.
Do not auto-delete code or imply deletion is safe without structural evidence.
Do not mark text-only or heuristic-only unused-code guesses as actionable removal findings.
Do not make dependency-cruiser, knip, an LLM, or any single external tool the sole authority for correctness-sensitive decisions.
Do not weaken existing P33 structural-correctness protections, false-positive controls, low-confidence grouping, or source-evidence requirements.
Do not require paid or runtime-only production coverage.

rows.evidence: rq-ss010 taxonomy is present in slop spec, command, skill, CATEGORIES/DEAD_CODE, and slop-smells; adv-slop-scan-assets test verifies all six deletion subtypes and targeted suite passed (73 tests).
rows.notes: Covers unused dependency/export/file, unreachable branch, uncallable private symbol, impossible feature-flag path.

rows.evidence: rq-ss011 deletion safety text routes uncertain deletion candidates to low-confidence/user-review; adv-slop-scan-assets test verifies command/skill/dead-code anchors and targeted suite passed.
Command, skill, DEAD_CODE, and rq-ss011 state heuristic-only/text-only unused-code guesses are not actionable removal proof; asset test assertion updated and passing.
rq-archstack01 and adv-arch-scan command/skill require stack packs before research/generic heuristic fallback; adv-arch-scan-assets tests passed.
rq-archstack02 ADV stack pack covers TypeScript/Bun/OpenCode plugin/Temporal/spec-command-skill assets; missing packs are reported via coverage.missingPacks; asset tests passed.
rows.notes: Initial ADV pack and missing-pack coverage documented.

rows.evidence: rq-ss012 and rq-archcov01 define normal text coverage summaries plus JSON coverage fields; asset tests verify skipped/degraded/missing coverage fields and passed.
rows.notes: Coverage visible without verbose; detail in JSON.

rows.evidence: .adv specs, docs mirrors, command/skill assets, and asset tests were updated; targeted suite passed (73 tests) and pnpm run check passed.
rows.notes: Includes new Phase 3 trigger asset test and doc version alignment.

rows.evidence: Existing rq-ss001/rq-ss004/rq-ss006/rq-ss007/rq-ss008/rq-ss009 contracts remain covered by asset tests; review found no weakening after remediation.
Changes update .adv/specs/slop-scan, .adv/specs/arch-scan, and docs mirrors; docs/specs/slop-scan.md version now matches spec.json 1.2.0.
rows.notes: Specs remain laws and mirrors aligned.

rows.evidence: Command/skill contracts require file/source/tool evidence for findings and structural owners for ADV stack-pack findings; review evidence cited file:line locations.
Deletion-safety sections list protected false-positive surfaces; context-boundary and low-confidence grouping asset tests passed.
Coverage gaps are required in normal text summaries; detailed fields live under JSON coverage for both scanner commands.
Arch scan stack-pack flow adds known packs and reports missing detected packs in coverage.missingPacks; Phase 3 standalone semantics and failure handling documented/tested.
Slop command, skill, DEAD_CODE, and spec state deletion candidates are review inputs only and never auto-deletion actions; review found no contrary guidance.
Heuristic-only/text-only unused-code guesses are explicitly non-actionable; low-confidence/user-review path verified by tests.
Slop deletion safety rejects a single external tool as sole authority; arch ADV stack pack cites tests/validators and treats tool output as evidence, not sole authority.
P33 structural-correctness requirements remain in both specs; asset tests for structural correctness, context boundary, and low-confidence grouping passed.
Scanner command/skill changes do not replace review, harden, audit, or tron workflows; commands remain scanner-specific contracts.
Implementation uses spec/command/skill/docs/asset-test contracts and optional detector reporting; no paid or production-only coverage is required.

Uncertain deletion candidates appear in a separate low-confidence/user-review section.
`/adv-slop-scan` never treats heuristic-only or text-only unused-code guesses as actionable removal proof.
`/adv-arch-scan` supports stack rule packs before generic heuristic fallback.
Initial packs cover ADV’s known stack; newly detected relevant stack pieces dynamically get a pack or explicit missing-pack coverage.
Skipped/degraded detectors and missing packs are always summarized, with detail in JSON/metadata.
